Title: Stop Describing Your Voice to the AI
Body1_P1: You've been adding adjectives to your ChatGPT prompts like seasoning a bad meal.
Body1_P2: More instructions will not fix it. You need a different approach entirely.
Body2_P1: Not by explanation. You can describe your tone for 500 words. It won't stick.
Body2_P2: It learns patterns from what you show it — not from what you tell it.
Body3_P1: Paste something YOU actually wrote. An email. A post. A newsletter.
Body3_P2: Then ask it to write the new thing in that style. That's it. Context beats instructions.
Body4_P1: They teach you clever tricks. Not YOUR voice. Everyone using the same prompts sounds the same.
Body4_P2: The prompt-pack industry sold you shortcuts. You needed a system.
Body5_P1: Build a voice sample library. Feed the AI your real writing before every task.
Body5_P2: Instructions set the task. Examples set the voice.
Takeaway:
STOP COLLECTING PROMPTS.
START FEEDING IT YOUR REAL WRITING.
CTA_Steps:
1) SAVE FOR LATER
2) COMMENT 'VOICE' TO GET MY FREE AI VOICE GUIDE
